ref: When they returned to the land of Canaan, they told their father Jacob everything that had happened to them:
text: Ni ame̱ba ame̱tše̱ Iakob ṅõ ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱, ni ame̱bagbale̱ nii ni afeame̱ le̱ fē ake̱:
translated: And Jacob dwelled in the land of Canaan, this place where his father was a stranger, and his descendants ate bread there:

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: ake̱-ni Abraham batšṍ dšeṅmaṅ kpeteṅkple ní he wa, ni abadšō̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ dšeṅmãdši fẽ ye̱ emli le̱?
translated: Then Abraham saw the place where he was to go; did he make a sacrifice there to God?

ref: Many years later a new king came to power. He did not know what Joseph had done for Egypt,
text: Agbene maṅtše̱ hẽ ko bate ši ye̱ Mizraim ní lee Iosef.
translated: Agbene sent him to Egypt where Joseph was.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: ni nyesā nyekē̱ale̱ ake̱: Na, otšũlo̱ Iakob nyie wo̱se̱. Edšake̱ ekē̱: Mike̱ nikēnii ni nyie mihĩe ne̱ yakpãtãle̱, ni no se̱ dãni mayana ehĩe, ekole̱ eekwe̱ mihĩe.
translated: This is the genealogy of Jacob. Jacob called his sons and said: My sons, come and listen to me, for I will tell you what will happen in the future and what will befall you in the days to come.

ref: Then the governor of Egypt told us, “I'll find out if you really are honest. Leave one of your brothers here with me, while you take the grain to your starving families.
text: Ni nū le̱ ni dši šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ nuṅtšo̱ le̱ kē̱wo̱ akē̱: Ene̱ maṅõ-le ake̱ anokwafoi dšinye: nyešĩa nyenyemimei le̱ ateṅ mokome nyehãami, ni hõmo̱ ní yo̱ nyešĩai le̱ hewo̱ le̱ nyeṅõa niyenii ke̱-yaã,
translated: I am with you and I will not abandon you; I will guide you and lead you, for I am your God and I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.
